page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-16-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the c4d server family. It features 16 dedicated virtual CPUs and 126.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 7.88 GB of RAM per vCPU. This configuration does not include any graphics processing units and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The dedicated CPU allocation ensures that compute resources are not shared with other tenants, providing consistent execution times for memory-intensive workloads. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio offers a dense memory footprint relative to the compute allocation, which is advantageous for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity without the overhead of additional CPU cores.

This server configuration is designed for workloads such as medium-sized databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise applications, and data processing tasks that demand significant memory capacity per core.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-16-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server instance from the c4d family, configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 126.0 GB of RAM. This resource allocation provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.88 GB per core, positioning the instance for memory-intensive operations. The hardware profile does not include integrated GPUs and offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. With its dedicated CPU allocation, the instance delivers predictable compute performance without resource contention. This server type is designed for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, data analytics, and enterprise applications that require a dense memory footprint relative to compute capacity.
